summary: Fiery New Speedway/Nobody's Fault jams and soaring Lovelight dominate
blurb: The set bursts from a brief acoustic opening into a high‑tempo electric blaze, highlighted by the blazing New Speedway → Nobody’s Fault → New Speedway sequence and a scorching Turn On Your Lovelight. Jerry’s guitar is punchy and his vocals aggressive, while the rhythm section drives with fire. Standout moments include Friend Of The Devil, Good Morning Little School Girl and the China > Rider pairing.
review: May 14, 1970 at Merramac Community College captures a young Dead in full‑tilt mode. After an unassuming acoustic opener, the band launches into a rapid‑fire electric second set that never lets up – the signature New Speedway → Nobody’s Fault → New Speedway jam roars through the night, feeding straight into a blistering Turn On Your Lovelight finale. Jerry Garcia is on fire, his Strat delivering scorching tone and an aggressive vocal bite that “punches you right in the guts,” while his pedal‑steel work adds a country‑flavored sparkle to the acoustic numbers. The rhythm section—Bill’s propulsive drumming complemented by Mickey’s later contributions and Phil’s solid, if occasionally thin, bass—keeps the momentum blazing. Bob Weir peppers the performance with classic banter that lifts the vibe. Highlights such as Friend Of The Devil, Good Morning Little School Girl, St. Stephen and a tight China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider showcase the band’s improvisational chemistry, making this show a must‑listen for fans of early‑’70 high energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six soundboard analyses are positive, with only one mixed review and no consistent negative commentary. Reviewers repeatedly praise the electric jams, Jerry’s guitar tone, and overall high‑energy performance, warranting a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with clear, listenable audio; highlights the New Speedway/Nobody's Fault jam and Lovelight without noticeable drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Scorching New Speedway → Nobody’s Fault → New Speedway jam that fuels the electric second set.
Turn On Your Lovelight closes the night with a blazing, high‑energy vibe.
Friend Of The Devil opens the show with fast‑paced drive, setting the tone for a rapid early ’70 performance.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers scorching guitar tone and aggressive vocals, punching through the jam sections; his pedal‑steel shines on acoustic numbers, with only occasional tuning wobble noted in one source.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid low end, though a few recordings note moments where the bass thins or briefly disappears.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds classic banter and humor throughout, contributing to the show’s relaxed yet high‑energy feel without any major musical criticism.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart joins on drums after the acoustic set, adding extra drive; his playing is energetic but not featured in the early portion of the concert.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann anchors the rhythm with a fiery, on‑fire drumming style that propels the fast‑paced early ’70 set.
